name: mine-shaft-entrance
summary: The surface head of a mine — a wooden headframe over the shaft, a winch to haul ore, sorting tables, and tool racks. The shaft itself drops into darkness at the center.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: squareish with the shaft dominating the center

accent.purpose: dusty ore-dump zone near sort tables

primary_palette.note: classic head-frame — shaft, winch, ore handling
industrial mine — larger operation with timber headframe
guarded mine — armed supervision, weapons at the entrance

story_prompts: shift change — miners emerging blackened, next shift descending
collapse — rescue winch hauling up an injured miner, foreman shouting for timbers
strike day — shaft silent, miners blocking the entrance, owner at the office door
rich vein — ore cart overflowing, foreman weighing a fist-sized nugget

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: dim half-outdoor light (0.3-0.4) with lanterns near the shaft

anti_patterns: shaft is a hole — treat it like a pit fill with wooden frame around it, not a prop floating on floor
ore carts on implied rails — don't scatter them randomly, they run in tracks to the sort table
headframe is tall — it's implied to extend upward, keep the shaft cells unobstructed
